natural_diversity.description: Shows 5 Q&A pairs then generates diverse queries for each
natural_diversity.template:
You are helping create test queries for a course FAQ search engine.

Here are 5 real FAQ entries from different courses:

{qa_pairs}

For EACH of the 5 questions above, generate 3 alternative search queries a student might type.

Rules:
1. SHORT (3-5 words): keyword search, typos allowed
2. PROBLEM-FIRST: describe symptoms the student sees
3. WRONG ANGLE: student doesn't know the real cause yet

Return ONLY a JSON object where keys are the FAQ question numbers (1-5) and values are arrays of 3 strings.

Example format:
{
  "1": ["query a", "query b", "query c"],
  "2": ["query d", "query e", "query f"],
  "3": ["query g", "query h", "query i"],
  "4": ["query j", "query k", "query l"],
  "5": ["query m", "query n", "query o"]
}

persona_based.description: Shows 5 Q&A pairs, generates persona-based queries for each
persona_based.template:
Here are 5 FAQ entries from a course platform:

{qa_pairs}

For EACH of the 5 questions above, generate 3 search queries from different students:
1. A beginner who doesn't know course terminology
2. A frustrated student describing symptoms they see
3. A lazy student typing quick keywords (2-4 words)

Return ONLY a JSON object with keys 1-5, each containing an array of 3 strings.

Example: {"1": ["beginner q", "frustrated q", "lazy q"], "2": [...]}

minimal.description: Shows 5 Q&A pairs, generates quick varied queries
minimal.template:
FAQ entries:

{qa_pairs}

For EACH question (1-5), write 3 different search queries:
1. Symptom-based: what they see going wrong
2. Wrong-angle: searching from their last action
3. Quick: terse keywords, 2-4 words

Return ONLY a JSON object: {"1": ["q1", "q2", "q3"], "2": [...]}
